title: Find missing divisors and dividends (1-digit division)
description: Find missing divisors and dividends in 1-digit division problems.  Quotients are less than or equal to 10.
topic_path: Module 3: Multiplication and division with units of 0, 1, 6–9, and multiples of 10
Topic C: Multiplication and division using units up to 8
Find missing divisors and dividends (1-digit division)

hints:
We can think of division as the following:

$\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}} =\purpleC{\text{total}} \div\greenD{\text{number of groups}} $

We have $\greenD7$ equal groups.

When we have $\maroonD{2}$ in each group, what is the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$?

$\maroonD{2}= {\purpleC{?}} \div \greenD{7}$

![2 equal number tapes showing ? is the same as 7 equal groups of 2](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e78928bd13efcab8daddc1e5f1dcbcea3434a234)

$\maroonD{2} \times \greenD{7} = \purpleC{14}$

The total is $\purpleC{14}$ units.

![2 equal number tapes showing 14 is the same as 7 equal groups of 2](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1df60914904f4e6d9da74c36c6dc0d5c86ce1b34)
$\maroonD{2}= \purpleC{14} \div \greenD{7}$
$\purpleC{?}=\purpleC{14}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{number of groups}} =\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$

We have $\greenD6$ equal groups.

When we have $\maroonD{4}$ in each group, what is the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$?

$\greenD{6}= {\purpleC{?}} \div \maroonD{4}$

![2 equal number tapes showing ? is the same as 6 equal groups of 4](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d3d1bc338ab35cd913e4802199286ebb6b651489)

$\greenD{6} \times \maroonD{4} = \purpleC{24}$

The total is $\purpleC{24}$ units.

![2 equal number tapes showing 24 is the same as 6 equal groups of 4](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5c5a1898fd6bcf1ae1b250dcd74dc929c51f1e59)
$\greenD{6}= \purpleC{24} \div \maroonD{4}$
$\purpleC{?}=\purpleC{24}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}$

We have $\greenD8$ equal groups.

When we have $\maroonD{2}$ in each group, what is the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$?

$\purpleC{?} \div \maroonD{2} = \greenD{8}$

![2 equal number tapes showing ? is the same as 8 equal groups of 2](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4be391bda7ab1ed01b959163e524622317189498)

$\greenD{8} \times \maroonD{2} = \purpleC{16}$

The total is $\purpleC{16}$ units.

![2 equal number tapes showing 16 is the same as 8 equal groups of 2](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bcea0d7bd35192d39b62ab263c044455216e1ffe)
$\purpleC{16} \div \maroonD{2} = \greenD{8}$
$\purpleC{?}=\purpleC{16}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}$

We have $\greenD3$ equal groups.

When we have $\maroonD{6}$ in each group, what is the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$?

$\purpleC{?} \div \maroonD{6} = \greenD{3}$

![2 equal number tapes showing ? is the same as 3 equal groups of 6](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ca1efa4b68b1b88a785ccea3db421fdc3abcccff)

$\greenD{3} \times \maroonD{6} = \purpleC{18}$

![2 equal number tapes showing 18 is the same as 3 equal groups of 6](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fb4a9595eba31c639e30e3fd7e4c043d72c57a13)
$\purpleC{18} \div \maroonD{6} = \greenD{3}$
$\purpleC{?}=\purpleC{18}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}=\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}}$

We have $\purpleC{10}$ total.

When we have $\maroonD{10}$ in each group, what is the $\greenD{\text{number of groups}}$?

$ \maroonD{10} = \purpleC{10} \div{} \greenD{?} $

![2 equal number tapes showing 10 is the same as ? equal group of 10](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d2efb5781d9b2a1a4c772c4fea71f2850be0f033)

$\greenD{1} \times \maroonD{10} = \purpleC{10}$

There is $\greenD{1}$ group.

![2 equal number tapes showing 10 is the same as 1 equal group of 10](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1758a3f4e91d155cd7513526650c47404189fd47)
$ \maroonD{10} = \purpleC{10} \div{} \greenD{1} $
$\greenD{?}=\greenD{1}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}=\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}}$

We have $\purpleC{18}$ total.

When we have $\maroonD{2}$ in each group, what is the $\greenD{\text{number of groups}}$?

$ \maroonD{2} = \purpleC{18} \div{} \greenD{?} $

![2 equal number tapes showing 18 is the same as ? equal groups of 2](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eeac29f35faf940eaf6bef198b8e7e91e29437be)

$\greenD{9} \times \maroonD{2} = \purpleC{18}$

There are $\greenD{9}$ groups.

![2 equal number tapes showing 18 is the same as 9 equal groups of 2](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b468492e7b0771e7bb28b662342e8ee9fef219c7)
$ \maroonD{2} = \purpleC{18} \div{} \greenD{9} $
$\greenD{?}=\greenD{9}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}= \greenD{\text{number of groups}}$

We have $\purpleC{12}$ total.

When we have $\greenD{4}$ equal groups, what is the $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$?

$\purpleC{12} \div{}\maroonD? ={} \greenD{4} $

![2 equal number tapes showing 12 is the same as 4 equal groups of ?](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0e5137b08e99de3f135066c1f1fefc3e8d2ee5ee)

$\greenD{4} \times \maroonD{3} = \purpleC{12}$

There are $\maroonD{3}$ in each group.

![2 equal number tapes showing 12 is the same as 4 equal groups of 3](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1a8a8abead4c6ea167152d32923cd39e9e90c9c)
$\purpleC{12} \div{}\maroonD3 ={} \greenD{4} $
$\maroonD{?}=\maroonD{3}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}$

We have $\greenD3$ equal groups.

When we have $\maroonD{9}$ in each group, what is the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$?

$\purpleC{\heartsuit} \div \maroonD{9} = \greenD{3}$

![2 equal number tapes showing a heart is the same as 3 equal groups of 9](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7862ac1efa145ca3f5170d374ec5a40dfa1d2a25)

$\greenD{3} \times \maroonD{9} = \purpleC{27}$

![2 equal number tapes showing 27 is the same as 3 equal groups of 9](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6e1fb995abb9be346187728787766404486d5ef2)
$\purpleC{27} \div \maroonD{9} = \greenD{3}$
$\purpleC{\heartsuit} = ~\purpleC{27}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}$

We have $\greenD4$ equal groups.

When we have $\maroonD{4}$ in each group, what is the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$?

$\purpleC{\clubsuit} \div \maroonD{4} = \greenD{4}$

![2 equal number tapes showing a clubsuit is the same as 4 equal groups of 4](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6e4cfde2f73d275ab6522c10ffd467983a8af8e7)

$\greenD{4} \times \maroonD{4} = \purpleC{16}$

$\purpleC{16} \div \maroonD{4} = \greenD{4}$
$\purpleC{\clubsuit} = \purpleC{16}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{number of groups}}= \purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}} $

We have $\greenD3$ equal groups.

When we have $\maroonD{6}$ in each group, what is the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$?

$ \greenD{3}=\purpleC{\bigstar} \div \maroonD{6} $

![2 equal number tapes showing a bigstar is the same as 3 equal groups of 6](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ced6c76967b57047e56219f5e6bf49aad9e38355)

$\greenD{3} \times \maroonD{6} = \purpleC{18}$

$ \greenD{3}=\purpleC{18} \div \maroonD{6} $
$\purpleC{\bigstar} = \purpleC{18}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}$

We have $\purpleC{16}$ total.

When we have $\greenD{8}$ equal groups, what is the $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$?

$\purpleC{16} \div{}\maroonD{\clubsuit} ={} \greenD{8} $

![2 equal number tapes showing 16 is the same as 8 equal groups of club suit](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/124807b02ec62ed469518a2798c2691d398c9d0c)

$\greenD{8} \times \maroonD{2} = \purpleC{16}$

$\purpleC{16} \div{}\maroonD{2} ={} \greenD{8} $
$\maroonD{\clubsuit} = \maroonD2$

We can think of division as the following:

$\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}$

We have $\purpleC{70}$ total.

We have $\greenD6$ equal groups.

We can think of division as the following:

$\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}}=\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}} $

We have $\purpleC{45}$ total.

When we have $\maroonD{9}$ in each group, what is the $\greenD{\text{number of groups}}$?

$\purpleC{45} \div{} \greenD{n}= \maroonD9 $

![2 equal number tapes showing 45 is the same as n equal groups of 9](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/801aa0b87de849fa5ea1d346600632d985c594a5)

$\purpleC{45} \div{} \greenD{5}= \maroonD9 $
$\greenD{n} = \greenD{5}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}} =\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}}$

We have $\purpleC{12}$ total.

When we have $\maroonD{3}$ in each group, what is the $\greenD{\text{number of groups}}$?

$\maroonD3 = \purpleC{12} \div{} \greenD{h}$

![2 equal number tapes showing 12 is the same as h equal groups of 3](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3e3a8c9a2b141e8ee6c98a0f9ef35294656a74c9)

$\greenD{4} \times \maroonD{3} = \purpleC{12}$

$ \maroonD3 = \purpleC{12} \div{} \greenD{4} $
$\greenD{h} = \greenD{4}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{number of groups}}=\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$

We have $\greenD7$ equal groups.

When we have a total of $\purpleC{7}$, what is the $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$?

$\greenD{7}= \purpleC{7} \div \maroonD{f}$

![2 equal number tapes showing 7 is the same as 7 equal groups of f](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/be0a85105db52c2ce9bfa06790426b4ef9686b41)

$\greenD{7} \times \maroonD{1} = \purpleC{7}$

![2 equal number tapes showing 7 is the same as 7 equal groups of 1](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5016b79fdd2019dcc51ae2f9fcbd7d779ab8a697)
$\greenD{7}= \purpleC{7} \div \maroonD{1}$
$ \maroonD{f} =  \maroonD{1}$

We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{number of groups}}=\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$

We have $\greenD2$ equal groups.

When we have a total of $\purpleC{8}$, what is the $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$?

$\greenD{2}= \purpleC{8} \div \maroonD{g}$

![2 equal number tapes showing 8 is the same as 2 equal groups of g](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/38a5e22c0bc68fd397625e2b2f7eb6d7a4b298c4)
